meta.title: Infection Control Plan — Respiratory Unit, Ward 4B
meta.subtitle: Transmission-based IPC plan (CDC isolation precautions / WHO IPC core components / Spaulding)
meta.author: HSE Healthcare
meta.deid_notice: All personnel and patients are referred to by role label. Cases and clusters are reported de-identified and aggregated, with categories of fewer than five suppressed and secondary suppression applied so a suppressed cell cannot be back-calculated from totals. Identifiers were pseudonymised before drafting; no patient infection status is attributed to a named person, and the re-identification key is held separately and is not part of this document.

sections.text: Respiratory Unit, Ward 4B. Agents in scope and their transmission routes were identified BEFORE control selection: a contact agent (multidrug-resistant organism), a droplet agent (influenza-like illness), and an airborne agent (suspected pulmonary tuberculosis). Surveillance case detail and any patient infection status were scrubbed to role/aggregate level before drafting; no named patient's infection status is carried into this circulated plan.
Engineering & administrative controls (applied BEFORE PPE — the hierarchy lever)

Controls-first: ventilation, single-room / negative-pressure isolation (AIIR), cohorting, screening and signage are applied BEFORE relying on PPE. An airborne agent managed by an N95/FFP3 respirator alone, with no airborne-infection isolation room or ventilation provision, is a FLAG pushed up the hierarchy — PPE is the residual barrier, never the headline control.

rows.control: Airborne-infection isolation room (negative pressure, monitored air changes per hour) for the airborne agent; ventilation upgrade where rooms are inadequate
Single rooms / spatial separation for droplet; dedicated single-room or cohorting + dedicated equipment for contact
Cohorting, early screening / triage, isolation signage, restricted entry, hand-hygiene programme + audit
Route-appropriate PPE as the residual barrier (gown+gloves contact; surgical mask droplet; fit-tested respirator airborne)

sections.text: PPE is NOT the primary control. A plan whose only airborne control is 'wear an N95/FFP3' with no negative-pressure isolation room, ventilation, or restricted-entry provision is a FLAG pushed up the hierarchy.
Transmission-based precautions by route (Standard Precautions always)

rows: Hand hygiene programme; safe injection; environmental cleaning
Airborne-infection isolation room (negative pressure); restricted entry; ventilation
sections.caption: Standard Precautions for every patient, then Transmission-Based precautions layered by route — engineering/administrative controls precede PPE (CDC isolation precautions / KB-SNIP-IPC-PRECAUTIONS)
Spaulding classification (KB-STD-IPC-CDC-WHO). A critical device reprocessed by high-level disinfection only — not sterilization — is a Spaulding mis-application and FAILS.

sections.text: A critical device (entering sterile tissue / the bloodstream) must be STERILIZED. High-level disinfection of a critical device is a Spaulding mis-application and is rejected.

items: IPC programme with a trained team and clear governance (WHO core component 1).
Evidence-based IPC guidelines + education and training (core components 2–3).
Surveillance of healthcare-associated infection, de-identified and aggregated (core component 4).
Multimodal improvement strategy + monitoring/audit and feedback (core components 5–6).
Workload, staffing, bed-occupancy and the built environment / materials (core components 7–8).

metrics.label: HAI cases this period (aggregated)
metrics.caption: small cells fewer than five suppressed; secondary suppression applied

metrics.label: Isolation-room negative-pressure verification
metrics.caption: daily log; gaps tracked at unit level

sections.text: Cluster and case counts are reported at unit/aggregate level only. Any category of fewer than five individuals is suppressed, and a second category is suppressed (or aggregated up) so the suppressed value cannot be back-calculated from a published total. No outbreak is reported on a named ward in a way that re-identifies a patient.

rows.title: Airborne agent controlled by respirator alone
rows.description: A draft plan relied on an N95/FFP3 respirator with no negative-pressure isolation room or ventilation provision; flagged and pushed up the hierarchy — an airborne-infection isolation room is the engineering control, PPE the residual barrier.

rows.title: Daily negative-pressure verification not logged
rows.description: The isolation-room negative-pressure log has weekend gaps; an automated alarm + log is recommended.

sections.text: After the engineering controls (AIIR / negative pressure, single rooms, ventilation), administrative controls (cohorting, screening, hand-hygiene audit) and route-appropriate residual PPE, the residual transmission risk is framed via risk_matrix as Low–Medium, with PPE as the documented last line.

rows.action: Commission daily negative-pressure verification with an automated alarm + log for the AIIR
Confirm the Spaulding reprocessing matrix per device class; sterilize all critical devices
Run the WHO IPC core-component self-assessment and close the gaps

items: Surveillance counts taken from the de-identified aggregated record; categories of fewer than five are suppressed — a missing figure is a [GAP], never invented.
India site: state biomedical-waste / IPC return owed — [GAP] (resolve the state via hse-india; BMW Rules 2016; no national form number minted).
CDC Guideline for Isolation Precautions — Standard + Transmission-Based (contact / droplet / airborne) precautions
WHO Core Components of Infection Prevention and Control Programmes
Spaulding classification — critical (sterilization) / semi-critical (high-level disinfection) / non-critical (low-level disinfection)
UK Health and Social Care Act 2008 Code of Practice on the prevention and control of infections (the Hygiene Code)
OSHA bloodborne pathogens standard — confidential handling of exposure / health data via KB-REG-OSHA-BBP
India Bio-Medical Waste Management Rules 2016 segregation via hse-india (no national form number)
ISO 45001 §6.1.2 — hazard identification & risk assessment; §8.1.2 — hierarchy of controls
Review trigger: annual IPC review / on-outbreak / on-guideline-change — whichever is first
Decision-support only — a competent person (infection-prevention & control professional) must review before use.
